texto: How hard it is to kill hope! Time after time, one thinks one has trodden it down, stamped it to death. Time after time, like a noxious insect, it begins to stir again, it shivers back again into a faint tremulous life. Once more it worms its way into one's heart, to instil its poison, to gnaw away the solid hard foundations of life and leave in their place the hollow phantom of illusion.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dorothy Bussy: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “How hard it is to kill hope! Time after time, one thinks one has trodden it down, stamped it to d…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, esperanca, illusion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e illusion.
